title: Business Attribute Data Release Policy | Stats NZ
content:
## Business Attribute Data Release Policy

## Purpose of the Policy
1. The purpose of this policy is to set out Stats NZ’s policy for releasing business attribute data.
2. Under section 39(2)(d) of the Data and Statistics Act 2022 , the Government Statistician may publish or disclose data about a business entity that they are satisfied is non-sensitive data, explicitly listing (but not limited to) business:
- industry and sector type it belongs to
- size indicators, e.g. number of employees.
3. Stats NZ’s release policy is that specified attribute data about a business entity will only be released to organisations and individuals where its use is for:
- producing official statistics (statistics produced by Stats NZ, other public sector agencies, or others approved by the Government Statistician to produce those statistics) or
- other approved public interest purposes (e.g. research in the public interest).
4. Stats NZ will not release business attribute data for purposes related to enforcement or regulation.

### Who this policy covers
5. This policy covers all business entities. In the context of this policy, a ‘business’ means an entity (whether incorporated or not) or a body of individuals that has any income or expenditure related to an economic activity (e.g., producing goods and services or engaging paid employees) within New Zealand. This can cover individual proprietors, partnerships, companies, central or local government agencies, trusts, and not-for-profit organisations.

### Specified attribute data about a business entity
6. Specified attribute data about a business entity includes:
- address of business (postal and physical address)
- contact details of the business entity
- numbers of persons engaged in the business
- industrial classification
- business type or structure
- sector type the business belongs to
- New Zealand Business Number (NZBN).
7. Stats NZ may release other business attribute data we assess to be non-sensitive on a case-by-case basis.
8. Business attribute data, in this context, does not usually include information relating to sole traders, as it is personal information.
9. Note: A sole trader is a self-employed person and is recognised and treated as an ‘individual’ under the Privacy Act 2020 . This means that we consider information related to sole traders as personal information and will not usually share data about sole traders.
10. We assess public interest using the following criteria:
- benefit to the public (e.g. improve quality and availability of data to drive better public services, decision-making and/or innovation)
- contribution to economic, social, cultural, and environmental well-being of Māori (including iwi and hapū)
- risk of harm to: Māori an individual or organisation public confidence in the protection of data.
- an individual or organisation
- public confidence in the protection of data.
- respondent load including cost of compliance for individual businesses and the aggregate effect for New Zealand.
11. All applications for release of business attribute data must include an assessment against the public interest criteria and an assessment of any likely impact on Māori. Ngā Tikanga Paihere is a framework for guiding ethical and culturally appropriate data use and may help to guide your assessment.

### Organisations must use the business attribute data in line with written agreement with Stats NZ
12. Where details of business entities are supplied under this policy, the organisation receiving those details will be required to provide assurances to Stats NZ that it will:
- not pass the data on to any third party
- only use the data for the purpose for which it was provided
- only retain the data until it is no longer needed.

### We publish approved releases of business attribute data
13. Stats NZ publishes information about approved releases of business attribute data including the name of the organisation, type of data, and how data was used on Stats NZ’s website .

### Tatauranga Aotearoa Stats NZ’s Te Tiriti obligations
14. Tatauranga Aotearoa Stats NZ’s Te Tiriti obligations arise from Te Tiriti o Waitangi/the Treaty of Waitangi, the Public Service Act 2020 and the Data and Statistics Act 2022, the latter of which specifically sets out the expectation upon Tatauranga Aotearoa Stats NZ, to recognise and respect the Crown’s responsibility to give effect to the principles of Te Tiriti o Waitangi/ the Treaty of Waitangi.
